title: calathea fasciata
intro: Calathea fasciata, also known as the Peacock Plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae, native to South America. The medicinal properties of Calathea fasciata include its use as an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds in its leaves. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve stress and anxiety, and even help lower blood pressure. The herbal preparations of Calathea fasciata typically involve infusions of the leaves, which can be consumed as a tea or used as a topical application to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
